source: FDA 2024 ADC Guidance; belantamab mafodotin BLA review
description: MMAF (monomethyl auristatin F) is a charged, membrane-impermeable auristatin with no bystander effect. Primary DLTs: thrombocytopenia and ocular keratopathy. Used in: belantamab mafodotin.

expected_findings.description: Thrombocytopenia (more severe than MMAE — charged metabolite lys-SMCC is more potent on megakaryocytes)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: PLAT <50% baseline with hemorrhage = adverse. Moderate (50-75% baseline) without hemorrhage is expected.
expected_findings.rationale: Charged MMAF metabolite (lys-SMCC) is more potent on megakaryocytes than MMAE. Primary DLT for MMAF class.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Any corneal finding beyond minimal epithelial changes = adverse. Dose-limiting clinically.
expected_findings.rationale: Corneal epithelial macropinocytosis of charged MMAF metabolites. Class-defining toxicity for MMAF ADCs.
expected_findings.translation_gap: Preclinical corneal findings may understate clinical severity. Ophthalmologic exams required.

expected_findings.description: Neutropenia (present but less prominent than MMAE)
expected_findings.rationale: Myeloid progenitor cytotoxicity. Less prominent than in MMAE due to lack of bystander effect.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Diffuse villous blunting with hemorrhage = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Expected class effect of microtubule inhibitor ADCs on intestinal crypt cells.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Complete alopecia or skin ulceration = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Cycling follicular keratinocytes sensitive to microtubule disruption.
